title: stylidium sejunctum
intro: Stylidium sejunctum, also known as the trigger plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Stylidiaceae. It is native to Australia and is known for its unique trigger-like mechanism that releases a chemical to deter herbivores when its leaves are touched. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Australian Aboriginal culture, to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The trigger plant can be prepared into herbal preparations such as teas or infusions to utilize its health benefits, which include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as treating skin conditions and respiratory issues.
